root_text.an3.132:1.3: Pāsāṇalekhūpamo puggalo, pathavilekhūpamo puggalo, udakalekhūpamo puggalo.
root_text.an3.132:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo abhiṇhaṁ kujjhati.
root_text.an3.132:1.6: So ca khvassa kodho dīgharattaṁ anuseti.
root_text.an3.132:1.7: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, pāsāṇe lekhā na khippaṁ lujjati vātena vā udakena vā, ciraṭṭhitikā hoti;
root_text.an3.132:1.8: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo abhiṇhaṁ kujjhati.
root_text.an3.132:1.9: So ca khvassa kodho dīgharattaṁ anuseti.
root_text.an3.132:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo abhiṇhaṁ kujjhati.
root_text.an3.132:2.3: So ca khvassa kodho na dīgharattaṁ anuseti.
root_text.an3.132:2.4: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, pathaviyā lekhā khippaṁ lujjati vātena vā udakena vā, na ciraṭṭhitikā hoti;
root_text.an3.132:2.5: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo abhiṇhaṁ kujjhati.
root_text.an3.132:2.6: So ca khvassa kodho na dīgharattaṁ anuseti.
root_text.an3.132:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo āgāḷhenapi vuccamāno pharusenapi vuccamāno amanāpenapi vuccamāno sandhiyatimeva saṁsandatimeva sammodatimeva.
root_text.an3.132:3.3: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, udake lekhā khippaṁyeva paṭivigacchati, na ciraṭṭhitikā hoti;
root_text.an3.132:3.4: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo āgāḷhenapi vuccamāno pharusenapi vuccamāno amanāpenapi vuccamāno sandhiyatimeva saṁsandatimeva sammodatimeva.
root_text.an3.132:3.6: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an3.132:1.1: “Mendicants, these three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.132:1.3: An individual like a line drawn in stone, an individual like a line drawn in sand, and an individual like a line drawn in water.
translation_text.an3.132:1.4: And who is the individual like a line drawn in stone?
translation_text.an3.132:1.5: It’s an individual who is often angry,
translation_text.an3.132:1.6: and their anger lingers for a long time.
translation_text.an3.132:1.7: It’s like a line drawn in stone, which isn’t quickly worn away by wind and water, but lasts for a long time.
translation_text.an3.132:1.8: In the same way, an individual is often angry,
translation_text.an3.132:1.9: and their anger lingers for a long time.
translation_text.an3.132:1.10: This is called an individual like a line drawn in stone.
translation_text.an3.132:2.1: And who is the individual like a line drawn in sand?
translation_text.an3.132:2.2: It’s an individual who is often angry,
translation_text.an3.132:2.3: but their anger doesn’t linger long.
translation_text.an3.132:2.4: It’s like a line drawn in sand, which is quickly worn away by wind and water, and doesn’t last long.
translation_text.an3.132:2.5: In the same way, an individual is often angry,
translation_text.an3.132:2.6: but their anger doesn’t linger long.
translation_text.an3.132:2.7: This is called an individual like a line drawn in sand.
translation_text.an3.132:3.1: And who is the individual like a line drawn in water?
translation_text.an3.132:3.2: It’s an individual who, though spoken to by someone in a rough, harsh, and disagreeable manner, still stays in touch, interacts with, and greets them.
translation_text.an3.132:3.3: It’s like a line drawn in water, which vanishes right away, and doesn’t last long.
translation_text.an3.132:3.4: In the same way, an individual, though spoken to by someone in a rough, harsh, and disagreeable manner, still stays in touch, interacts with, and greets them.
translation_text.an3.132:3.5: This is called an individual like a line drawn in water.
translation_text.an3.132:3.6: These are the three individuals found in the world.”

variant_text.an3.132:3.2: sandhiyatimeva → … yeva (sya-all, km); … ceva (pts1ed) | saṁsandatimeva → … yeva (sya-all, km); … ceva (pts1ed) | sammodatimeva → … yeva (sya-all, km); … ceva (pts1ed)
